```{r}
library(MASS)
library(np)

# Load your RData file
load("/Users/yangjinzhe/Desktop/doctorvisits.rda")

# Suppose your object is called 'df' (data.frame)
write.csv(doctorvisits, "doctorvisits.csv", row.names = FALSE)

tail(doctorvisits)
model <- glm(numvisit ~ reform + badh + age + educ + loginc, family = poisson, doctorvisits)

model_nb <- glm.nb(numvisit ~ reform + badh + age + educ + loginc, doctorvisits)

# model result for poisson regression
model
p_res <- residuals(model, type = "pearson")
sum(abs(p_res) > 3)

# model result for negtive binomial regression
model_nb
p_res <- residuals(model_nb, type = "pearson")
sum(abs(p_res) > 3)

theta <- 0.9978731512246424
mu <- 3.742388660271744
dnbinom(0, size = theta, mu = mu)
```


```{r}
summary(model_nb)

bw_cond <- npcdistbw(numvisit ~ factor(reform) + factor(badh) + age + educ + loginc,
    data = doctorvisits,
    wmethod = "cv.ml"
)

bw_cond
```

```{r}
tail(predict(model_nb), )

c(1, 1, 0, 38, 7, 7.72323894500732) %*% c(-0.407475, -0.137408, 1.131247, 0.005570, -0.005320, 0.137129)
```

```{r}
model
tail(predict(model, type = "response"))
linearCom <- c(1, 1, 0, 38, 7, 7.72323894500732) %*% c(-0.421508, -0.139884, 1.132628, 0.004885, -0.011814, 0.152025)
linearCom
exp(linearCom)
```

<!-- Conditional distribution data (2227 observations, 6 variable(s))
(1 dependent variable(s), and 5 explanatory variable(s))

                        numvisit
Dep. Var. Bandwidth(s): 1.257102
                        factor(reform) factor(badh)      age      educ   loginc
Exp. Var. Bandwidth(s):      0.4845307   0.01094098 5.786841 0.4327276 213813.7

Bandwidth Selection Method: Least Squares Cross-Validation
Formula: numvisit ~ factor(reform) + factor(badh) + age + educ + loginc
Bandwidth Type: Fixed
Objective Function Value: 0.1854022 (achieved on multistart 2)

Continuous Kernel Type: Second-Order Gaussian 
No. Continuous Dependent Vars.: 1 
No. Continuous Explanatory Vars.: 3 

Unordered Categorical Kernel Type: Aitchison and Aitken  
No. Unordered Categorical Explanatory Vars.: 2 -->
